confidenceLevels.high: Directly confirmed against a primary source; no material ambiguity that this record is correct and current.
confidenceLevels.moderate: Confirmed, with a minor gap or a single secondary corroboration; the curator is reasonably certain.
confidenceLevels.low: A single weak source or a hard-to-read artefact; the record is recorded but should be treated with caution.
confidenceLevels.very-low: Fragmentary evidence only; the curator records the value honestly as uncertain.

evidenceLevels.high: A strong regulatory position or systematic-review-grade evidence underpins the claim.
evidenceLevels.moderate: A single authoritative opinion or consistent secondary evidence underpins the claim.
evidenceLevels.low: Limited or indirect evidence underpins the claim.
evidenceLevels.very-low: Only mechanistic or anecdotal evidence underpins the claim.
